senses.definition: to put food into the mouth, then chew and swallow it

examples.x: They enjoy eating pizza on Friday nights.
He was too busy to eat lunch due to back-to-back meetings.
We ate sushi for the first time and loved it.
It's essential to eat a balanced diet for overall health.
The kids were so hungry after playing outside that they couldn't wait to eat dinner.

senses.definition: to cause food, drink, or another substance to pass from the mouth down into the stomach, using the muscles of the throat

examples.x: It's important to chew your food well before you swallow.
She took a sip of water and then tried to swallow the large pill.
The baby is learning how to swallow solid foods.
Inhaling too quickly may cause you to swallow air, leading to discomfort.
The baby hesitated before finally swallowing the mashed banana.

senses.definition: to eat or drink something

examples.x: Let's have breakfast together before we start our day.
Would you like to have a cup of tea with me?
I usually have lunch at the cafeteria near my office.
I had a sandwich for lunch today.
She likes to have a smoothie for breakfast.
They had pizza for dinner last night.
Let's have a snack while we watch the movie.
He had a glass of water to quench his thirst.

senses.definition: to eat or drink something

examples.x: After a long day at work, she was ready to consume a hearty meal.
It's important to consume a balanced diet that provides essential nutrients for overall health.
As part of the celebration, the family gathered to consume a delicious feast.
The picnic menu included a variety of dishes that everyone consumed with delight.
In the cozy café, patrons consumed hot beverages and freshly baked pastries.

senses.definition: to be able to recognize the flavor of something by eating or drinking it

examples.x: She tastes the savory herbs in her homemade soup.
As the wine connoisseur swirled the glass, he anticipated the opportunity to taste the complex notes of the aged red wine.
If you try this exotic fruit, you will taste a unique combination of flavors.

senses.definition: to lightly or minimally eat or taste a small portion of something

examples.x: She's on a diet, so she'll probably just touch the main course.
The chef was curious why the customer had barely touched the special dish.
He touched the new dish cautiously, unsure if he'd like it.
As a sign of appreciation, he would touch each dish on the tasting menu.

senses.definition: to test something by doing or using it to find out if it is suitable, useful, good, etc.

examples.x: Have you tried the new flavor of ice cream at the shop?
He tried a new shampoo to see if it worked better.
He tried sushi for the first time and found it delicious.
She tried the new workout routine and found it challenging.

senses.definition: to consume a drug, medication, or substance in a specified manner, such as swallowing, inhaling, or injecting

examples.x: The nurse instructed the patient to take the prescribed antibiotics with a full glass of water.
The pharmacist explained how to take the pain medication — one pill every four hours as needed.
The doctor advised him to take the cough syrup every six hours until the symptoms improved.
She hesitated before deciding to take the flu shot to protect herself from the seasonal virus.
The asthmatic patient needs to take the inhaler as soon as they experience shortness of breath.
The athlete was careful to take the prescribed dose of the performance-enhancing drug as directed by the sports doctor.
The recovering addict struggled not to take any illicit substances during the rehabilitation process.

senses.definition: to take food, drink, or another substance into the body by swallowing or absorbing it

examples.x: Animals often ingest various plants and herbs as part of their diet in the wild.
Some cultures have specific rituals associated with the way they ingest certain traditional dishes.
The doctor advised the patient to ingest the prescribed medication with a full glass of water.
Birds use their beaks to capture and ingest small insects as a primary source of protein.
During the experiment, participants ingested a controlled amount of the test substance to measure its effects.

senses.definition: the act of eating, drinking, or utilizing edible items for sustenance or pleasure

examples.x: The rapid consumption of snacks during the movie was noticeable.
Mindful consumption of fruits and vegetables is encouraged for a balanced diet.
The festival celebrated the cultural diversity of food through the consumption of traditional dishes.
Excessive consumption of sugary drinks can lead to health issues.
The event had delicious gourmet food for everyone's consumption.

senses.definition: to have dinner

examples.x: The couple decided to dine at their favorite restaurant for their anniversary.
She prefers to dine early in the evening to avoid the crowds.
We often dine with friends on weekends, enjoying good food and conversation.
Last night, they dined at a fancy restaurant to celebrate their achievements.

senses.definition: to have a meal, typically at home or in a specified location, rather than going out to eat at a restaurant

examples.x: We decided to dine in and enjoy a homemade dinner tonight.
Let's order takeout and dine in, avoiding the crowded restaurant.
She loves to cook, so they often prefer to dine in rather than dine out.

senses.definition: to have dinner in a restaurant or at someone else's home
to have a meal at home, in contrast to eating at a restaurant or ordering takeout

examples.x: After a long day at work, she preferred to eat in and enjoy a homemade dinner.
Instead of dining out, the family decided to eat in and share a meal together.
She planned to eat in for the week to save money and explore new recipes.

senses.definition: to eat in a restaurant, etc. rather than at one's home

examples.x: On special occasions, the family likes to eat out at their favorite restaurant.
After a busy day, she decided to treat herself and eat out instead of cooking.
The couple enjoys exploring new cuisines and frequently chooses to eat out.
Instead of packing lunch, they decided to eat out and try the nearby cafes.
When traveling, it's common for tourists to eat out and experience local cuisine.

senses.definition: to eat until one is completely satisfied

examples.x: The buffet at the party had so much food that I couldn't help but fill up.
After the long hike, we were ravenous and decided to fill up on a hearty meal.
Don't fill up on appetizers; the main course is going to be fantastic.

senses.definition: to complete the entire portion of a meal

examples.x: Even though the portions were large, she managed to finish her entire meal.
The children were determined to finish their vegetables before dessert.
Despite feeling full, she couldn't resist the tasty dish and decided to finish it.
Please make sure to finish your lunch before heading out for the afternoon.
We encouraged everyone to finish their dinner as there were delicious desserts waiting.

senses.definition: to successfully swallow or ingest food or drink

examples.x: She had to chew her food thoroughly to ensure it would get down smoothly.

senses.definition: to allow oneself to do or have something that one enjoys, particularly something that might be bad for one

examples.x: She decided to indulge in a piece of chocolate cake as a treat.
He often indulges in late-night snacks, even though he knows it's not good for his health.
They like to indulge in a luxurious spa day every once in a while to relax and unwind.
I occasionally indulge in shopping sprees, even though I know I should save money.
We indulged in a weekend getaway to the beach to escape the stresses of everyday life.

senses.definition: to eat snacks or light meals

examples.x: While waiting for the train, travelers would nosh on grab-and-go sandwiches and salads from the station cafe.
The evening gathering included a spread of tapas for guests to nosh on while socializing.

senses.definition: to eat lunch, particularly at a restaurant
to have a meal early in the morning
to participate in consuming food
to finish eating something completely, often quickly or with enthusiasm

examples.x: After the party, he polished off the leftover cake in one sitting.
She polished off her plate of spaghetti, savoring every bite.
They polished off the entire tray of nachos while watching the game.
He managed to polish off two large slices of pizza in no time.
The kids polished off their snacks before heading outside to play.
She polished off the last of the cookies, leaving none for anyone else.

senses.definition: to consume a drink or liquid food

examples.x: She sat by the fireplace, quietly supping on a cup of hot tea to warm herself on the chilly evening.
The elderly gentleman preferred to sup his soup slowly, savoring each spoonful for its comforting warmth.
The child happily supped on a glass of milk while listening to bedtime stories.
As the sun sets, the friends gather around the campfire to sup on mugs of hot cocoa.
The artist takes breaks from painting to sup on a refreshing fruit smoothie.

senses.definition: to cut into flesh, food, etc. using the teeth

examples.x: The dog warned the intruder by growling before attempting to bite.
The toddler cried loudly after accidentally biting his own finger.
To catch its prey, the predator would often bite with precision.
The toddler tried to bite into the juicy apple but struggled with his small teeth.
He couldn't resist the temptation and decided to bite into the tempting chocolate bar.

senses.definition: to consume food quickly and without taking the time to chew it thoroughly

examples.x: Hungry and in a rush, he bolted his breakfast, barely tasting the food.
The students, eager to get to their next class, bolted their snacks during the short break.
Noticing the approaching storm, the campers had to bolt their lunch to seek shelter quickly.
The busy executive often had to bolt his meals in between meetings and conference calls.
Faced with a tight schedule, the athlete had to bolt a protein bar before the race.

senses.definition: used to wish someone a good appetite or enjoyable meal before they start eating
to chew energetically or noisily

examples.x: He started to champ on the crunchy popcorn at the movies.
The hungry dog began to champ on the delicious bone.
The toddler champed happily on the soft cookie.
She couldn't resist champing on the crispy potato chips.
The goat eagerly champed on the hay in the feeding trough.
The cows champed their cud lazily in the field.
He champed his gum loudly, annoying those around him.

senses.definition: to eat or swallow something with difficulty or reluctance

examples.x: He tried to choke down the overcooked meal to be polite.
They managed to choke down the unappetizing lunch to avoid wasting food.
He struggled to choke down the dry sandwich during the lunch meeting.
The challenge was to choke down the spicy dish without water.

senses.definition: to chew or bite down on something with a strong, audible, and repeated motion

examples.x: The hungry toddler couldn't wait to chomp the crunchy apple, revealing a toothy grin with each bite.
As the popcorn movie night began, everyone in the room started to chomp their buttery popcorn.
The enthusiastic eater couldn't resist the temptation to chomp the juicy watermelon slices at the summer picnic.
When the crunchy chips were brought out at the party, guests began to chomp them while engaging in conversation.

senses.definition: to bite and crush food into smaller pieces with the teeth to make it easier to swallow

examples.x: He chews his food slowly to aid digestion.
The cows chew cud in the peaceful meadow.
She has already chewed the pencil out of nervousness.

senses.definition: to crush or grind something loudly and noisily with the teeth

examples.x: He crunched the potato chips loudly during the movie.
She crunched the hard candy in her mouth, savoring the flavor.
The dog crunched the biscuit eagerly.
She crunched the popcorn while watching the show.

senses.definition: to eat something with a lot of enjoyment and finish it all

examples.x: He demolished the pizza in no time.
The kids managed to demolish the entire cake.
She can demolish a plate of spaghetti effortlessly.
We decided to demolish the tacos at the food truck.
Despite the large portion, he was able to demolish the burger.

senses.definition: to eat something eagerly and in large quantities, often implying intense hunger or enjoyment

examples.x: The aroma of freshly baked cookies made it irresistible, and the kids eagerly devoured the entire batch.
At the summer barbecue, guests lined up to devour the delicious grilled burgers and hot dogs.
With excitement, the family sat down to devour the Thanksgiving feast they had spent hours preparing.
After a day of hiking, they are so hungry that they can devour a hearty meal in no time.
In the bustling food market, visitors eagerly devour street food from various vendors.

senses.definition: to eat small amounts or particular kinds of food, especially to lose weight

examples.x: She decided to diet by cutting out sugary snacks and eating more vegetables.
He started to diet to prepare for the upcoming marathon.
After the holidays, many people choose to diet to shed the extra pounds.
The doctor advised her to diet by following a low-carb plan.
They both decided to diet together, supporting each other through the process.

senses.definition: to start eating with enthusiasm

examples.x: As the delicious aroma filled the air, everyone was eager to dig in and enjoy the homemade feast.
The kids dug in, devouring their plates in minutes.
She took one look at the dessert and dug in immediately.
They dug in as soon as the barbecue was ready.
The family gathered around the table and dug in together.

senses.definition: to rapidly and completely consume food

examples.x: He downed the whole pizza in under 10 minutes.
They downed their meals before heading out to the meeting.
He downed three burgers at lunch.

senses.definition: to consume completely, especially in reference to food

examples.x: The hungry children quickly ate up all the cookies that were on the table.

senses.definition: to eat something quickly and greedily, often making loud and rapid swallowing sounds

examples.x: The children tend to gobble their candy as soon as they get it on Halloween.
During the picnic, everyone gobbled sandwiches and chips.
In a rush, she had to gobble her lunch before the meeting.

senses.definition: to swallow quickly or greedily, often in one swift motion

examples.x: In the competition, participants were challenged to gulp a glass of milk as quickly as possible.
The child couldn't wait to gulp down the refreshing lemonade on a hot day.
The dog eagerly gulped the treat offered by its owner.
Trying not to be late, he had to quickly gulp down his breakfast.

senses.definition: to drink something, especially an alcoholic beverage, enthusiastically, and in large quantities

examples.x: Thirsty after the game, he proceeded to guzzle a whole bottle of water.
The friends decided to guzzle sodas while watching the movie marathon.
At the celebration, they began to guzzle champagne in high spirits.
After the long hike, they sat around the campfire and guzzled hot cocoa.
The crowd started to guzzle cold beer as they enjoyed the live music.

senses.definition: to pass the tongue over a surface, typically to taste or eat something

examples.x: The cat stretched out its tongue to lick its paw and clean its fur.
The toddler licked the spoon after finishing her yogurt.
He licked the stamp and stuck it onto the letter.
She licked the envelope to seal it before mailing it.
He licked his lips in anticipation of the delicious meal.

senses.definition: to chew steadily or vigorously, often making a crunching sound

examples.x: During the movie, we sat on the couch and munch on popcorn.
The cow leisurely munched on the grass in the meadow.
At the campfire, we sat around munching marshmallows.
The toddler sat in the high chair, happily munching on small pieces of apple.
During the meeting, he discreetly munched his way through a bag of almonds.

senses.definition: to eat small amounts of food often

examples.x: She prefers to nibble throughout the day instead of having big meals.
He nibbled at his desk, enjoying small bites of his lunch.
She likes to nibble on cheese and grapes while watching TV.

senses.definition: to nibble or eat small amounts of food in a hesitant or cautious manner

examples.x: The toddler would peck at the vegetables on his plate, preferring the pasta.
She decided to peck at the salad, saving room for dessert.
Not feeling very hungry, she opted to peck at the snacks during the party.
Instead of devouring the meal, he chose to peck at it slowly.
The cat would peck at its food, taking small bites at a time.

senses.definition: to eat only a small amount of food

examples.x: She just picked at her food during dinner, claiming she wasn't hungry.
If you keep picking at your meals, you won't get enough nutrients.
Despite ordering a large plate, he only picked at it and barely ate anything.
Children often pick at vegetables they don't like.
She's been picking at her meals ever since she started that diet.

senses.definition: to fully appreciate and enjoy the flavor or aroma of a food or drink as much as possible, particularly by slowly consuming it

examples.x: She savors the delicate flavors of herbal teas.
Right now, I am savoring the warmth of freshly brewed coffee.
The food critic is savoring each bite to provide a detailed review.
They were savoring the local delicacies during their culinary tour.
He paused to savor the delicious taste of the freshly baked cookies.

senses.definition: to eat or drink quickly or eagerly

examples.x: He scarfs his breakfast every morning before heading to work.
The children scarfed down the pizza at the birthday party.

senses.definition: to eat a small amount of food between meals, typically as a quick and informal meal

examples.x: During the movie, they like to snack on popcorn and enjoy the film.
The kids often snack on crackers and cheese after school.
While working on the project, they took a break to snack on nuts and energy bars.
On road trips, it's common for people to snack on chips and other convenient treats.
To curb their hunger before dinner, they snacked on hummus and vegetable sticks.

senses.definition: to transfer or serve food using a spoon, typically involving scooping or lifting with kitchen tools

examples.x: He will spoon the soup into the bowl for lunch tomorrow.
They have spooned mashed potatoes onto their plates already.
We had spooned out servings of ice cream for dessert before the party started.
Don't forget to spoon the gravy over the turkey before dinner is served.
She has carefully spooned the batter into the muffin cups several times.

senses.definition: to eat something quickly and voraciously

examples.x: The ravenous predator decided to wolf the prey it had caught, devouring it with precision.
The competitive eater managed to wolf a staggering number of hot dogs in record time during the contest.
When the buffet opened, hungry attendees began to wolf the delicious spread of international cuisines.
The enthusiastic foodie couldn't help but wolf the gourmet burger with delight at the trendy restaurant.
The camping trip brought out the adventurer's appetite as they set up the campfire to wolf a simple yet satisfying meal.

senses.definition: to eat and drink abundantly, often as part of a celebration or special occasion

examples.x: The villagers would often feast on local delicacies during their annual harvest celebration.
In the ancient tradition, the tribe feasted on the successful hunt, sharing stories around the fire.
After the victory, the team feasted on pizza and drinks to celebrate their championship.
To mark the special occasion, the company decides to feast on a lavish dinner at an upscale restaurant.

senses.definition: to drink or eat excessively

examples.x: After the stressful week, he decided to binge on his favorite comfort foods.
During the movie marathon, they couldn't resist binging on popcorn and snacks.
Stressful situations sometimes lead people to binge on chocolate or other treats.
The teenager tended to binge on energy drinks during exam periods.
Some individuals may binge on fast food as a way of coping with emotional distress.

senses.definition: to eat excessively, especially to the point that makes one feel sick or uncomfortable
to eat greedily and in large quantities

examples.x: After fasting all day, she decided to gorge on a hearty meal at the buffet, savoring each dish.
The aroma of freshly baked pastries in the bakery caused customers to stop and gorge on sweet treats.
As the barbecue smoke wafted through the air, friends eagerly gathered to gorge on grilled burgers and ribs.
In the aftermath of the successful harvest, the community came together to gorge on a bountiful harvest feast.
At the all-you-can-eat seafood buffet, diners gorged on a variety of ocean delights.

senses.definition: to consume a large quantity of food

examples.x: I can't believe how much pizza he can pack away.
She packed away three servings of spaghetti at dinner.
He was so hungry, he packed away the whole sandwich in minutes.

senses.definition: to eat a lot and quickly, often in a greedy or indulgent way

examples.x: Despite having a full meal, he continued to pig on snacks throughout the movie.
Trying not to pig during the holiday feast proved challenging for her.
Feeling stressed, she chose to pig on chocolate to find comfort.
Unable to resist the tempting aroma, they began to pig on the freshly baked cookies.

senses.definition: to eat or drink noisily by inhaling a liquid or soft food, such as soup or noodles, often with a distinctive, impolite sound

examples.x: He couldn't help but slurp his noodles loudly, causing a few raised eyebrows in the restaurant.
The children giggled as they slurped their ice cream floats through colorful straws.
Trying the hot ramen for the first time, she attempted to slurp the noodles just like they do in Japan.
At the dinner table, my brother always manages to slurp his soup, much to the annoyance of our parents.
The comedian on stage pretended to slurp his coffee loudly for comedic effect.
